string.c(3) Library Functions Manual string.c(3)

NAME

string.c - 🔤 String manipulation utilities: ASCII escaping, trimming, case conversion, and formatting

SYNOPSIS

Macros


#define COLORED_BUFFERS 4
#define COLORED_BUFFER_SIZE 256

Functions


void escape_ascii (const char *str, const char *escape_char, char *out_buffer, size_t out_buffer_size)
Escape ASCII characters in a string. bool validate_shell_safe (const char *str, const char *allowed_chars)
bool escape_shell_single_quotes (const char *str, char *out_buffer, size_t out_buffer_size)
Validate that a string contains only safe characters for shell commands. bool escape_shell_double_quotes (const char *str, char *out_buffer, size_t out_buffer_size)
Escape a string for safe use in shell commands (double quotes, Windows-compatible). bool string_needs_shell_quoting (const char *str)
bool escape_path_for_shell (const char *path, char *out_buffer, size_t out_buffer_size)
Check if a string needs shell quoting (contains spaces or special chars). const char * colored_string (log_color_t color, const char *text)
Build a colored string for terminal output. void truncate_with_ellipsis (const char *input, char *output, size_t output_size, int max_width)
Truncate a string to a maximum display width, with ellipsis. void strip_ansi_codes (const char *input, char *output, size_t output_size)
Strip ANSI escape codes from a string.

Detailed Description

🔤 String manipulation utilities: ASCII escaping, trimming, case conversion, and formatting

Definition in file util/string.c.

Function Documentation

bool string_needs_shell_quoting (const char * str)

Definition at line 219 of file util/string.c.

219                                                  {
220   if (!str || str[0] == '\0') {
221     return false; // Empty strings don't need quoting
222   }
223 
224   // Check for characters that need shell quoting
225   for (size_t i = 0; str[i] != '\0'; i++) {
226     char c = str[i];
227 
228     // Whitespace characters always need quoting
229     if (c == ' ' || c == '\t' || c == '\n' || c == '\r') {
230       return true;
231     }
232 
233     // Shell special characters
234     if (c == '"' || c == '\'' || c == '$' || c == '`' || c == '\\' || c == '<' || c == '>' || c == '&' || c == ';' ||
235         c == '|' || c == '(' || c == ')' || c == '[' || c == ']' || c == '{' || c == '}' || c == '*' || c == '?' ||
236         c == '!' || c == '~' || c == '#' || c == '@') {
237       return true;
238     }
239   }
240 
241   return false;
242 }




Referenced by escape_path_for_shell().






bool validate_shell_safe (const char * str, const char *
  allowed_chars)


Definition at line 62 of file util/string.c.


62                                                                      {
63   if (!str) {
64     return false;
65   }
66 
67   // Shell metacharacters that are dangerous
68   // ; & | $ ` \ " ' < > ( ) [ ] { } * ? ! ~ # @ space tab newline
69   const char *dangerous = ";|$`\\\"'<>()[]{}*?!~#@ \t\n\r";
70 
71   // First pass: validate UTF-8 encoding
72   if (!utf8_is_valid(str)) {
73     return false;
74   }
75 
76   // Second pass: validate each character for shell safety
77   // Convert to codepoints and check only ASCII range for dangerous characters
78   // (non-ASCII characters are allowed by default)
79   const uint8_t *p = (const uint8_t *)str;
80   while (*p) {
81     uint32_t codepoint;
82     int decode_len = utf8_decode(p, &codepoint);
83     if (decode_len < 0) {
84       return false; // Should not happen after utf8_is_valid check
85     }
86 
87     // Only validate ASCII range where shell metacharacters exist
88     // Non-ASCII characters (codepoint > 127) are allowed
89     if (codepoint <= 127) {
90       unsigned char c = (unsigned char)codepoint;
91 
92       // Allow ASCII alphanumeric characters
93       if (isalnum(c)) {
94         p += decode_len;
95         continue;
96       }
97 
98       // Check if character is in allowed_chars list
99       if (allowed_chars) {
100         bool is_allowed = false;
101         for (size_t j = 0; allowed_chars[j] != '\0'; j++) {
102           if (c == (unsigned char)allowed_chars[j]) {
103             is_allowed = true;
104             break;
105           }
106         }
107         if (is_allowed) {
108           p += decode_len;
109           continue;
110         }
111       }
112 
113       // Check if character is dangerous
114       bool is_dangerous = false;
115       for (size_t j = 0; dangerous[j] != '\0'; j++) {
116         if (c == (unsigned char)dangerous[j]) {
117           is_dangerous = true;
118           break;
119         }
120       }
121       if (is_dangerous) {
122         return false;
123       }
124     }
125 
126     p += decode_len;
127   }
128 
129   return true;
130 }






References utf8_decode(), and utf8_is_valid().


Referenced by gpg_get_public_key().
